headline: A play-in-your-browser story game that's dressed up to look like a coding terminal.
what_it_does: You open a web page and it acts like an old-style computer terminal with a chat partner named VERA. You type (or click) commands to poke around a made-up workplace and uncover a five-chapter story, with quiet sound effects and visual flourishes. Everything happens inside that one web page. It's a game, not a real tool, and it changes nothing on your actual computer.
runs_things: a local web page in your browser — nothing else starts or runs

risks.plain: It quietly saves your game progress and a couple of settings (sound on/off, text speed) inside the browser's own private storage — not real files on your PC — so it changes nothing outside the browser and clears if you clear browsing data.
